Yahya Rahim Safavi, senior military advisor to Iran's 🇮🇷 Supreme Leader: -Ayatollah Khamenei instructed him in 2011 to prepare a plan to close the Strait of Hormuz -Safavi worked with then-IRGC commanders Ali Fadavi, Amir Ali Hajizadeh, and Hossein Salami to draft a plan covering military operations in the Persian Gulf, the Sea of Oman, the Red Sea, and the Mediterranean -In 1997, when Safavi became IRGC commander, Khamenei placed particular importance on expanding Iran's missile arsenal. At teh time, Iran posessed ~2,000 missiles; Khamenei reportedly instructed military commanders to build and stockpile a missile arsenal capable of sustaining "at least three months of an offensive war."
